---
title: "3.3 Data storage"
subtitle: "Overview · Hardware P119-P120"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.76em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.32em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 3 Directory / 返回 Unit 3 目录](../../index.html)

## 3.3 Data storage

### Memory and storage

All computers require some form of <span class="term">memory</span> (内存) and <span class="term">storage</span> (存储).

<span class="term">Memory</span> is usually referred to as the internal devices used to store data that the computer can access <span class="mark">directly</span>. This is also known as <span class="term">primary memory</span> (主存储器). This memory can be the user's workspace, temporary data or data that is key to running the computer.

<span class="term">Storage devices</span> allow users to store applications, data and files. The user's data is stored <span class="mark">permanently</span> and they can change it or read it as they wish.

## Why Storage Is Needed

### Transfer and data loss protection

Storage needs to be larger than internal memory since the user may wish to store large files such as music files or videos.

Storage devices can also be <span class="mark">removable</span> to allow data, for example, to be transferred between computers.

Removable devices allow a user to store important data in a different location in case of <span class="term">data loss</span> (数据丢失).

However, all of this removable storage has become less important with the advent of technology such as <span class="term">data drop</span> (数据投送) and <span class="term">cloud storage</span> (云存储).

## Figure 3.57

### Typical memory and storage devices

![Figure 3.57 Typical memory and storage devices](../../assets/3-hardware/3-3-data-storage-overview-p119-p120/Figure 3.57 Typical memory and storage devices.png){fig-align="center" width="88%"}

## Two Distinct Groups

### Primary memory and secondary storage

Memory and storage devices can be split up into <span class="mark">two distinct groups</span>:

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="term">primary memory</span> (主存储器)
- <span class="term">secondary storage</span> (二级存储)
:::

## Figure 3.58

### Summary of primary and secondary devices

![Figure 3.58 Summary of primary, secondary and off-line devices](../../assets/3-hardware/3-3-data-storage-overview-p119-p120/Figure 3.58 Summary of primary, secondary and off-line devices.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

The key distinction is whether a device is <span class="mark">directly addressable by the CPU</span>.

## Classroom Check

### Keep the distinction precise

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- that <span class="term">memory</span> refers to internal devices that the computer can access <span class="mark">directly</span>
- that this is also known as <span class="term">primary memory</span>
- that storage is used to hold applications, data and files <span class="mark">permanently</span>
- that memory and storage devices are split into <span class="mark">primary memory</span> and <span class="mark">secondary storage</span>
- that the key difference is whether the device is <span class="mark">directly addressable by the CPU</span>
:::

## Bridge

### Next: 3.3.1 Primary memory

The next decks explain <span class="term">primary memory</span>, including <span class="term">RAM</span>, <span class="term">DRAM</span>, <span class="term">SRAM</span> and <span class="term">ROM</span>.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 3 Directory / 返回 Unit 3 目录](../../index.html)
